compiled_state.title: PRECONDITIONS degradation channel + sprint story-closure forcing function
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context

Sibling to Epic c183-ed2a (inference channel; this epic was split off at scrutiny review on 2026-04-26). DSO planning and execution skills contain "graceful degradation" prose paths — explicit branches that detect a precondition failure and continue execution rather than halting. These currently fall through silently with no structural record, so a downstream stage cannot tell whether an upstream check actually ran or skipped.

The 3dae-af1a self-attestation incident (CLI_user-tagged, open) is the canonical failure mode. In 3dae-af1a, a brainstorm orchestrator emitted a comment claiming a scrutiny rationale had been logged, when in fact no red-team-reviewer dispatch had occurred — there was no structural artifact (ticket comment, signal, attestation field) that a downstream check could fail on. The bypass shape is "agent asserts a procedural outcome the agent itself was responsible for producing." This epic adds the structural channel that makes such bypasses visible: every graceful-degradation fallthrough writes an event the downstream stage can refuse to proceed past.

## Glossary

- PRECONDITIONS: event log defined by epic 736d-b957 (closed). JSONL append-only artifact under the ticket-tracker tree, written by ticket-lib.sh._write_preconditions, read via preconditions-validator-lib.sh. This epic extends the event payload with optional degradation:bool field.
- decisions_log: array within a PRECONDITIONS event payload recording decisions made during a planning step (rationale, source, affects_fields). The degradation field added by SC1 is on entries of this array.
- minimal tier: lightest validation profile of preconditions-validator-lib.sh. Minimal-tier consumers must continue to function when the optional degradation field is absent.
- 818-corpus: historical fixture of 100 closed bugs at tests/fixtures/818-corpus/sample-bugs.json (described fully in sibling Epic c183-ed2a). Spike 5 (2026-04-27) used representative excerpts from the 11 surface skill files to baseline keyword-detector false-positive rates.
- degradation_type: discriminator field on degradation events distinguishing C1 (inferred-decision graceful-degradation: classifier unavailable, fall back to default) from C2 (unresolved-question graceful-degradation: gap analysis incomplete, proceed with partial context). SC6 sample-ack scopes by this field.
- Referenced ticket IDs: 736d-b957 (closed; PRECONDITIONS schema/writer); c183-ed2a (sibling; provides decision_id format and INFERENCE_SKIP envelope this epic reuses); 6068-cb2d (completion verifier, consumes SC1 degradation field at story closure); 6111-fc7f (structural sentinel; this epic's SC2 surface includes approval-gate.md which 6111-fc7f mechanically verifies); dso-94ya (sprint preflight; SC4 extends this surface); 3aaa-0238 (CI gates; SC3 hook plugs through); e884-d0ad (PostToolUse hook for sub-agent attention loss; adjacent SC2 hook infrastructure).

## Approach

Foundation → consumers in parallel, with one sequencing invariant: any change to the PRECONDITIONS event contract after foundation lands requires a re-foundation pass.

Spikes 3, 4, and 5 (2026-04-27) collapsed the original Story 0 spike requirements: SC2 marker mechanism uses existing bash-block prior art (sprint/SKILL.md lines 53-56) — no new hook needed; SC4 uses existing _read_latest_preconditions API on the orphan tickets branch — no marker file or harvest-sync needed; SC3 uses staged-diff scanning per existing pre-commit pattern (check-portability.sh, check-shim-refs.sh) — no whole-file FP problem.

Story 1 (foundation): PRECONDITIONS schema null-coalescing extension + degradation_type discriminator + ack event format. Story 2 (parallel): instrument 11 prose-path graceful-degradation sites with bash blocks calling _write_preconditions plus HTML comment landmarks. Story 3 (parallel): sprint Step 10a unacked-degradation pre-dispatch check. Story 4 (parallel): SC3 staged-diff keyword-detector pre-commit hook. Story 5: dual-channel ack + sample-ack. SC5 + SC6 must land in the same release because they jointly define the ack contract consumed by 6068-cb2d.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 12 (all accepted; analysis covered combined epic before 2026-04-26 split — both halves share the scenario evidence)
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: scenario analysis run once; fidelity review run three times across combined → split-A → split-B revisions
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: triggered — depth: 5 spikes (A-Verification-1, A-Spike-1, B-Spike-1 SC2 marker, B-Spike-2 SC4 harvest-sync, B-Spike-3 SC3 keyword detector)
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: triggered — cycles: 3, gap: SC2 marker mechanism, SC4 harvest-sync, SC3 semantic detector all flagged unverified or infeasible in early revisions; resolved via spikes that revealed structural simplifications (existing bash pattern, orphan branch reads, staged-diff scanning)
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: skill file modifications (epic instruments 11 planning + execution skill files with bash blocks)
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: not applicable (no volume decision)

## Success Criteria

SC1: PRECONDITIONS schema (writer in 736d-b957 story a96b-7ba8) gains two optional fields on decisions_log entries: degradation:bool and degradation_type:string (values inferred_decision | unresolved_question). Minimal tier ignores both. Legacy events without the fields are equivalent to {degradation:false, degradation_type:null} (null-coalescing semantics). Consumers MUST use null-coalescing reads, enforced by a behavioral test in the contract test suite that runs against a fixture containing legacy + new event shapes. Part C consumer manifest deliverable: enumerate every PRECONDITIONS consumer (completion-verifier, harvest-worktree.sh attest reader, sprint validator, any reviewer agent parsing PRECONDITIONS) and confirm each handles the absent-field case correctly. Manifest path: plugins/dso/docs/precondition-degradation-consumers.md.

SC2: Each of the 11 fallthrough sites in planning + execution skills (brainstorm/SKILL.md, brainstorm/phases/approval-gate.md, preplanning/SKILL.md, implementation-plan/SKILL.md, debug-everything/SKILL.md, fix-bug/SKILL.md, sprint/SKILL.md, sprint/prompts/auto-resume.md, sprint/prompts/test-failure-dispatch-protocol.md, validate-work/SKILL.md, shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md) contains a bash block that calls _write_preconditions with degradation:true and a degradation_type discriminator at the exact orchestrator-narrative point where the fallthrough decision is taken. The bash block follows the existing pattern at sprint/SKILL.md lines 53-56. Each bash block is preceded by an HTML comment annotation of the form: an EMIT-PRECONDITIONS comment landmark naming the gate, degradation flag, and type — as a grep-able landmark for SC3's detector. The annotation is documentation only; the bash block is the action. NO NEW HOOK is required (Spike 3, 2026-04-27): _write_preconditions and the _dso_pv_* family are the centralized writer. Per-site smoke validation: during instrumentation, one fallthrough site's bash block is exercised end-to-end (forced fallthrough → confirm PRECONDITIONS event written with correct degradation:true + type) at the per-story Step 10a-equivalent execution context (NOT the Phase 8 stage-boundary path which is already instrumented). Part C consumer enumeration deliverable: grep audit across plugins/dso/{skills,hooks,scripts,docs} and tests/ for any reference to the EMIT-PRECONDITIONS landmark string; produce manifest at plugins/dso/docs/precondition-marker-consumers.md listing every match. Coordination with 6111-fc7f: when both epics modify approval-gate.md, the later-landing epic rebases. Sentinel insertion (6111-fc7f) and degradation-marker insertion (this epic) occupy non-overlapping textual regions.

SC3: Pre-commit / CI check uses the existing DSO diff-scanning hook pattern (see check-portability.sh, check-shim-refs.sh, pre-commit-test-quality-gate.sh). The check scans STAGED DIFFS, not whole files — pre-existing prose is untouched, only new additions are scrutinized. Trigger keywords (constrained set per Spike 5 FP-baseline 2026-04-27, "proceed" and "skip" excluded due to 269+171=440 raw matches across the 11 target files): "fall through", "safe fallback", "log a warning and continue", "continue without", "fall back", "graceful degradation". Detection rule: when a staged diff in any of the 11 planning + execution skill files ADDS lines (not modifies existing) containing any trigger keyword, the hook checks whether an EMIT-PRECONDITIONS comment landmark (the SC2 marker) was also added within N=10 lines of the trigger. If no marker is found in the same diff hunk, the hook fails with an instructive message. Suppression annotation (the established DSO pattern, e.g., a precondition-emit-ok comment with a reason) bypasses the check on a per-line basis. FP rate on the staged-diff approach is bounded by the size of new additions per commit. The whole-file FP-baseline of 53 raw matches (constrained set) is irrelevant because the check never scans pre-existing lines. NO Story 0 spike required (Spike 5 confirmed the staged-diff approach inherits the proven low-FP pattern from existing pre-commit hooks). Boundary with 3aaa-0238 (CI-enforced quality gates): this hook owns PRECONDITIONS degradation prose detection only. 3aaa-0238 owns the generic CI enforcement infrastructure that this hook plugs into. Integration: this hook MUST register through the gate manifest defined by 3aaa-0238 if 3aaa-0238 ships first; otherwise wires directly into pre-commit and is migrated when 3aaa-0238 lands. Implementation pattern: bash script at plugins/dso/hooks/check-precondition-emit.sh following the shape of plugins/dso/hooks/check-portability.sh.

SC4: Sprint stage-boundary validator (existing surface from 736d-b957 story 92a6-52d6; coordinated with dso-94ya's preflight harness) gains an unacknowledged-degradation check at Step 10a, placed BEFORE the existing OPEN_CHILDREN check at sprint/SKILL.md:1829-1846, as a peer pre-dispatch prerequisite to dso:completion-verifier dispatch. The check is a new bash block that sources ticket-lib.sh and reads PRECONDITIONS events for the story via _read_latest_preconditions, counting entries with degradation:true that lack a paired ack event (defined in SC5). A story cannot transition to closed while any unacked degradation entry exists. Pre-manifest exemption: when _read_latest_preconditions returns no events for the story (legacy ticket), the check trivially passes. Cross-worktree race window: the check uses the same orphan-branch read mechanism as the existing OPEN_CHILDREN check at Step 10a, which has been in production since v3 ticket system rollout (epic 736d-b957) without observed race incidents (Spike 4, 2026-04-27); accepted-as-known. NO Story 0 spike required (Spike 4 confirmed the orphan-branch read path; no marker file or harvest-sync needed). Boundary with dso-94ya: SC4 extends the sprint validator surface dso-94ya owns. The unacknowledged-degradation check is added as a new check inside dso-94ya's preflight harness if dso-94ya lands first; otherwise SC4 ships standalone and dso-94ya integrates the check on landing.

SC5: Acknowledgment is dual-channel: (a) CLI: a new dso preconditions-ack subcommand taking a decision_id and a required --if-skipped="<concrete consequence>" — validated non-empty after trim, >=10 characters, must reference content from the named precondition. Algorithm (Spike specifies): normalized 3-word-window substring match using pure bash, mirroring the prior art at plugins/dso/hooks/lib/fuzzy-match.sh's _fuzzy_normalize_var. Pre-normalize the precondition text ONCE up-front, producing a normalized word array; slide the 3-word window over the normalized array (array slice + concatenation only — no per-window character normalization). Performance acceptance: 500-word precondition matched against a 50-word ack rationale completes in <=2 seconds (feasibility benchmark measured 0.35s; 5.7x under target). Language scope: ASCII / Latin scripts; non-Latin precondition text falls back to a manual-review path (gate refuses with explicit message; out of automatic-validation scope). (b) Interactive sprint prompt at story closure presents unacknowledged degradations; user types ack <decision_id> --if-skipped="..." per entry. Validation identical to CLI. Each ack event enumerates the specific decision_ids being absorbed (no implicit class-future absorption). Ack events written today never absorb degradations emitted after the ack timestamp. Audit precision: a periodic sample of 20 ack events shows >=80% contain a substantive (non-tautological, non-whitespace) --if-skipped rationale, judged by a documented rubric at plugins/dso/docs/contracts/ack-rationale-rubric.md.

SC6: For classes with >=4 entries, sample-ack --class=<degradation_type> mode randomly selects 3 entries from the class, requires per-entry --if-skipped for those 3, and absorbs the remainder ONLY when all 3 spot-checks have distinct, content-referencing rationales (same validation as SC5). Determinism: the ack record stores the sampled set (the 3 specific decision_ids selected) at sample time. The validator checks that all entries in the stored sampled set are acked — not "any 3 acks from the class." This avoids the failure mode where re-sampling with a different random seed would invalidate a prior valid ack. There is no class-level blanket ack. Scrutiny rationale (bot-psychologist 2026-04-26): blanket ack-all --class reproduces the 3dae-af1a self-attestation failure mode in a different shape.

## Dependencies

Depends on: c183-ed2a (sibling inference channel; provides decision_id format and INFERENCE_SKIP envelope shape — coarse dependency: this epic fully blocked until c183-ed2a closes).

Depends on (structural prerequisite, documented but not link-tracked due to closed-target restriction): 736d-b957 (PRECONDITIONS schema/writer infrastructure — closed).

Blocks: 6068-cb2d (completion verifier coordination SC reads SC1 degradation field).

Relates to: 6111-fc7f (structural sentinel — coordination on approval-gate.md; coordination comment posted 2026-04-26); dso-94ya (sprint preflight — SC4 surface coordination); 3aaa-0238 (CI gates — SC3 hook integration via gate manifest); e884-d0ad (PostToolUse hook for sub-agent attention loss — adjacent SC2 hook infrastructure); ca48-c3c3 (closed); d63f-2c74 (operational atomicity check); 5df7-63ac (observability — may consume PRECONDITIONS events for telemetry); 853a-c719 (preplanning observability — same); 736d-b957 (foundation, closed).

## Out of Scope

LLM-assisted semantic detection of graceful-degradation prose (deferred to a follow-up epic if SC3 staged-diff keyword approach proves insufficient post-deploy). Class-level blanket ack — explicitly rejected on scrutiny grounds (recreates 3dae-af1a). Non-Latin precondition text content-reference validation (manual-review fallback only). The inference-channel adversarial review work (lives in sibling Epic c183-ed2a). PRECONDITIONS schema/writer infrastructure (already in 736d-b957). Truths/artifacts/key_links verifier work (lives in 6068-cb2d).
